title: router training record
description: Canonical contract for one labeled router training pair (00-DESIGN.md 'Canonical training-record schema'). Every exported row MUST validate. Three field groups: input features, label group, provenance/OPT-055 stamp. pred_* guess fields are optional so fallthrough rows (no pred_*) and prefill rows (with them) both validate against this one contract. Legacy rows carrying the old qwen_* keys are normalized to pred_* on read (broker.router_train.aggregate._normalize_record) BEFORE validation, so the 604 pre-rename rows still validate.

prompt.description: Input feature: full untruncated user message the router saw.

messages.description: Input feature: the full system+user array the model actually saw (captures the OPT-001-buggy system prompt verbatim).

system_prompt_sha256.description: Input feature: hash of the rendered system prompt.

label_persona.description: Label group: the correct persona (= dispatched_persona). MUST NOT be 'general-purpose' (Claude default, not a Nexus persona) — such rows are dropped before export.

label_confidence.description: Label group: 1.0 sidecar/human, ~0.8 transcript-mined, lower on low-agreement.

labeled_at.description: Label group: timestamp the label was attached.

pred_persona.description: The router model's own guess for the persona (optional). Legacy rows carry this under qwen_persona; it is normalized to pred_persona on read.

agree.description: Whether the model guess (pred_persona) equals the label (label_persona).

schema_version.description: Provenance: separates v1 (the 604 legacy rows) from v2.

router_version.description: Provenance: OPT-001 quarantine flag. 'buggy' rows MUST be excluded from training.

model_id.description: Provenance: router model identity (granite-4.1-3b) — the ONLY field carrying model identity; kills pred_*/granite identity drift.

source_project.description: Provenance: which install produced the row (fleet provenance).

router_code_sha.description: Provenance: git sha of the router code at write time.

threshold.description: Provenance: the live THRESHOLD_LLM value at write time.

prompt_hash.description: Provenance: sha256(prompt) hex — stable id for idempotent dedupe.
